rulings.Case Title: DAWN N HICKS AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE MAGGIE LEE HICKS FAMILY TRUST et al VS. WELLS FARGO N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 10, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled in part, and sustained in part. Demurrer is overruled as to first cause of action for Breach of Contract. Plaintiff pleads the legal effect of the contract and any ambiguities can be clarified in discovery. Demurrer is overruled as to third cause of action for Negligence. For purposes of this demurrer, duty sufficiently pled as to account holder. Demurrer is overruled as to eighth cause of action for Elder Abuse. Plaintiff qualifies as the legal representative of Hicks who meets the definition of "elder." Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for Negligent Misrepresentation. Plaintiff must allege with greater particularity the substance of mispresentations and or specifiy the Brochures, advertisements, and other disclosures that made those representations. =(302/CWW/DC)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN QUILLINAN et al VS. NEW COLLEGE OF LAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 10, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT LOIS SCHWARTZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Schwartz's demurrer sustained without leave to amend. No facts alleged that Schwartz made mispresentations which induced plaintiff to enroll or continue his studies at New College. Plaintiff admits he suffered no damages as a result of New College's loss of accreditation, and no facts pled that defendant had any role in New College's dissemination of information to prospective students. Plaintiffs have not alleged a contract between themselves and defendant to imply the existence of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Facts alleged do not show arbitrary and capricious grading and testing. No facts alleged showing defendant's involvement with fraudulent advertising and civil conspiracy to obtain federal loans. The statements allegedly made by defendant are not strong enough to support a finding of intentional infliction of emotional distress. No reasonable probability that the defects can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW/PL)

rulings.Case Title: SHANEL STASZ VS. ESTATE OF HUGO W. QUACKENBUSH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 10, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT ESTATE OF HUGO W. QUACKENBUSH'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff is suing the estate, which is permissible under Probate Code section 550 when the decedent was protected by insurance. Summons shall be served on a person disignated in writing by the insurer or, if none, on the insurer. (Probate Code section 552.) No agency relationship shown between Eisenberg and the insurance company. (See Pelayo vs. City of Downey (2088) 570 F.Suup.2nd 1183.) =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ARIANE DOBRIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitrator; Nomination Of Judicial Arbitration And Mediation Services (Jams); Supporting Memorandum Of P/A; Declaration Of Hal Chase, Jr.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 10, 2009, line 15, PETITIONER ARIANE DOBRIN'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitrator; Nomination Of Judicial Arbitration And Mediation Services (Jams). Pursuant to CCP section 1281.6, the Court nominates the following five persons as potentional arbitrtors: Honorable Robert B. Yonts, Honorable V. Gene McDonald, Matthew White, Ted Shapiro, and Michael Ornstil. Counsel are to meet and confer regarding the selection of an arbitrator. If counsel cannot agree, they must appear at the hearing. =(302/CWW)
